feedingProfile.preferredNpk: half-strength low-nitrogen succulent fertilizer, growing season only (RHS: once or twice per season)

commonIssues: Root rot from overwatering — let soil dry fully between waterings and keep almost dry in winter
Leaf scorch from hot direct sun — prefers bright light with protection from intense midday sun
Fading of the white leaf banding/pattern under inadequate light
Leaf spot and fungal disease if foliage stays wet or soil is kept soggy

source.citation: North Carolina Extension Gardener Plant Toolbox — Haworthiopsis attenuata (Zebra Haworthia): allow soil to dry between waterings; bright indirect light; well-draining cactus/succulent mix; USDA zones 9-11; thrives 60-80 F, avoid below 50 F; rosette clumping habit; root rot, leaf spot, scale, mealybugs

University of Wisconsin-Madison Horticulture Extension — 'Haworthias: Super Succulents for Small Spaces': water weekly in summer, less in winter, soil dry between waterings; a few hours of direct light in east/west window plus bright indirect; clusters fit 4-5 inch pots, shallow pots preferred, do not oversize; optimal 75-90 F, tolerates low 40s to low 100s F, no frost; potting soil cut 1:1 with perlite/grit/pumice

RHS — Haworthia attenuata (zebra plant): grow in cactus compost in full light with protection from hot sun; water moderately in growth, keep almost dry in winter (dormant period); half-strength low-nitrogen fertiliser once or twice in the growing season; hardiness H2 (tolerates 1-5 C, will not survive freezing); mealybugs; overwatering is the primary risk

University of Wisconsin-Madison Horticulture Extension — light guidance (a few hours direct sun daily plus bright indirect; tolerates lower light than most succulents) used to set target/min/max sun hours

RHS hardiness H2 (1-5 C minimum) and UW-Madison tolerance range (low 40s F to low 100s F) used for coldHardyMinC=4 and heatStressC=38; frost-sensitive per both sources and NC State (zones 9-11, not frost tolerant)

Confidence note: sources give qualitative guidance, not exact numbers. baseIntervalDays=14 is a conservative reading of UW-Madison 'weekly in summer, less in winter' combined with universal 'let soil dry fully / overwatering is the primary risk' warnings. feedingProfile.baseIntervalDays=90 converts RHS 'once or twice per growing season'. Container liters converted from UW-Madison 4-5 inch pot guidance (~0.5-2 L). Sun hours estimated from qualitative 'few hours direct + bright indirect'. Treat these four fields as estimated.
